threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the overground' when talking about the trains. That'll change soon enough.
Heard a guy on the N35 bragging about taking the last bus just to avoid his ex at the pub. Classic Brixton move. Always a night story.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a family of squirrels raiding the Turkish bakery’s outdoor seating for crumbs—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you get late-night currywurst served with a side of techno beats. It’s like a culinary rave on the street!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to identify the exact moment when BART or Muni is going to screw up your commute—like a sixth sense.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that was more crowded than a Saturday market. It stopped twice because the driver couldn't hear the stops over the chatter.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares and nightlife, but they miss the tiny bakeries with the best pa de pessic tucked away on the side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they order a café con leche—always looking for the sugar first, like it’s a dessert.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses run on time after 10 PM? Welcome to Clapton, where the 253 is basically a myth after dark.
Caught the N3 last night and overheard a guy bragging about his wild night at the Windmill, but he was actually just talking up a bus stop argument.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street musician playing a tuba outside the Kebap shop at midnight, and everyone just danced around him like it was normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you hear a techno bass line while waiting for a bus that’s already late—makes every commute feel like part of the nightlife.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to double-check BART schedules before making dinner plans—those delays can ruin your whole night.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 7 bus, practicing his speech while clutching a bouquet. Only in the Tenderloin do you see pre-wedding jitters on public transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close early. Meanwhile, the locals know where to find the secret late-night spots that stay buzzing till dawn.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewildered expression at the Mercat de Sant Antoni when trying to navigate the layout.
